subject: Geography
title: The 49th Parallel
description: A short lesson to help you apply The 49th Parallel.

hook.text: The US-Canada border follows the 49th parallel for 2,000 km—a perfectly straight line.

buildup.text: In 1818, diplomats picked a latitude line because they couldn't agree on anything else.

discovery.text: Surveyors spent decades cutting a 6-meter-wide strip through forests to mark the line.

twist.text: The border splits towns, farms, and even a library—half in the US, half in Canada.

climax.text: It's the longest undefended border in the world—8,891 km with no wall or fence.

punchline.text: The world's longest border is defended by nothing but trust.

quiz.question: What is special about the US-Canada border?
options: It is the longest undefended border in the world
It was drawn after a war
